query: Benchmark the brief for our notification system.

context.preconditions: A complete BRIEF.md exists describing a real-time notification system with web push, in-app, and email channels. The brief includes an Assumptions & Risks section.

expected_behavior: Skill locates and reads docs/features/notification-system/BRIEF.md in full before starting research

Skill uses WebSearch to research 3-5 comparable solutions (e.g., OneSignal, Firebase Cloud Messaging, Pusher, Knock) and presents each with what it does, relevant approach, key differentiators, and gaps
Skill identifies 3-8 technical standards relevant to the domain (e.g., Web Push API, WCAG notification guidelines) and 3-6 key metrics with baselines
Skill writes a Gap Analysis that checks the brief's assumptions against research findings and flags contradictions
Skill writes BENCHMARK.md to the same directory as the BRIEF with a 'Generated from:' reference line and confirms each section interactively

query: Do a competitive analysis for the search feature.

context.preconditions: No BRIEF.md exists anywhere in the project. No docs/features/ directory matches 'search'. The user does not specify a file path.

expected_behavior: Skill searches for BRIEF.md in docs/features/*/BRIEF.md and the current working directory without finding one
Skill asks the user: 'Which BRIEF.md should I use? I did not find one in the current directory.'
Skill does not proceed with benchmark generation until a valid BRIEF.md is located or the user provides content
Skill does not fabricate a brief or assume feature details without a source document

query: Research competitors for our dashboard redesign.

context.preconditions: BRIEF.md describes a frontend analytics dashboard feature with UI components, charts, and data tables. DECISION.md contains accepted decisions from the create-brief phase. The feature is clearly frontend.

expected_behavior: Skill detects this is a frontend feature (based on UI, dashboard, component keywords in the brief) and includes the Visual References section
Skill reads DECISION.md and treats all accepted decisions as hard constraints throughout the benchmark
Skill produces Unicode box-drawing layout diagrams for competitor dashboards, captioned as reference material (not proposed designs)
Skill produces Mermaid user flow diagrams for competitor workflows, captioned as observed flows
Skill marks any unverified claims with the warning marker and flags conflicts between accepted decisions and research findings
